name: Saudi Arabia
summary: Day 40. SADARA $20B ARAMCO-DOW JV DESTROYED — IRGC retaliation strike on April 7 hit Jubail using missiles + drones. Sadara Tadawul: rated capacity to zero overnight (1.5Mt ethylene, 750Kt PE, 330Kt propylene oxide, 400Kt MDI). Largest single piece of Saudi industrial damage of the war. SABIC Jubail = 11% Saudi non-oil GDP / 6-8% global petrochemical output. King Fahd Causeway (Bahrain link) closed indefinitely. Saudi MoD intercepted 7 ballistic missiles. Pre-ceasefire war absorbed 7+ days of consecutive Eastern Province threats. Saudi Tadawul $TASI 11,262 (-0.1%) Tuesday — closed on Sunday Wed open will react to ceasefire. Yanbu bypass remains operational but ~40 VLCCs anchored 5-day waits.

signals.content: Saudi missiles intercepted. OPEC+ agreed to modest 206K bpd increase. Saudi already boosted production by 640K bpd ahead of conflict.
Saudi revived ~50% of oil exports via Yanbu Red Sea bypass. East-West pipeline operating at ~80% capacity. 4.2M bpd shipped from Yanbu.
Iran attacked Yanbu port with drone, briefly halting loadings. Ballistic missile intercepted. Even the bypass route is under threat.

Iran struck Prince Sultan Air Base (outside Riyadh) with 6 ballistic missiles and 29 drones on March 28 — 15+ US service members injured including 5 seriously, some aircraft damaged. This is the most significant attack on US forces at a Saudi facility in the war. Marks a shift in Iranian targeting of Saudi Arabia: from oil infrastructure (Yanbu Mar 18) to US military installations. Air raid sirens activated across Saudi Arabia, Kuwait, and Bahrain early Sunday March 29. Total US wounded in the war past 300. (Sources: NPR, Fortune, Al Jazeera, PBS, Washington Post, Stars and Stripes)

Saudi Ministry of Defence reported intercepting 5 ballistic missiles heading towards Eastern Province on Monday. Eastern Province is home to Saudi Aramco HQ, Ras Tanura terminal, and key refining infrastructure. Separately, the Islamabad quad diplomats (Saudi FM Prince Faisal bin Farhan among them) departed after Sunday consultations — Monday continuation did not happen. Pakistan still preparing to host 'meaningful talks.' (Sources: Al Jazeera liveblog, GMA News)

Jeddah Trilateral Summit (MBS + Qatar Emir + Jordan's King Abdullah, March 31) condemned 'Iranian attacks on vital and civilian facilities' — blaming Iran, not US/Israel. GCC: 'attack on one is threat to all, asserts right to self-defense.' Arab News: Saudi 'told Iran not to attack it, warns of possible retaliation.' Privately, Gulf allies pushing Trump to KEEP fighting (WaPo) — UAE described as 'most hawkish,' pushing for ground invasion. Saudi intercepted 5 Iranian missiles targeting Eastern Province (March 30). TASI +0.9% today, +7.4% 3M — oil exporter outperforming. CONFIRMED.

WaPo national security desk: Gulf allies privately urging Trump to continue fighting 'until Iran is decisively defeated.' UAE described as 'perhaps the most hawkish' pushing ground invasion. Kuwait and Bahrain also favor continued action. Saudi position conflicted: simultaneously suffering from war (missile attacks, infrastructure damage) AND lobbying for more war — seeing 'historic opportunity to cripple Tehran's clerical rule.' Bloomberg separately: 'MBS's Iran War Nightmare: Missiles, Chaos, Threaten Vision 2030' — suggesting Saudi's position more conflicted than WaPo alone suggests. TASI +0.7% — muted response to peace signal, consistent with market understanding Saudi doesn't want war to end quickly. TASI +7.2% 3M — oil exporter outperforming. Al-Salmi tanker attack near Dubai — Greek container ship also hit off Ras Tanura (Saudi port). REPORTED (WaPo anonymous sourcing). (Sources: WaPo, Bloomberg, Arab News, yfinance)

Saudi Arabia expelled Iranian military staff (persona non grata), citing Article 51 self-defense and UNSC Resolution 2817. This effectively reverses the 2023 Beijing Agreement — the China-brokered Saudi-Iran detente that was a landmark diplomatic achievement. Major escalation in Saudi posture from conflicted to openly hostile. Jeddah Trilateral Summit (Qatar Emir, MBS, King Abdullah) produced joint condemnation of Iranian strikes. WaPo reports Gulf allies privately pushing Trump to 'keep fighting until Iran decisively defeated.' Separately, ~40 VLCCs anchored outside Yanbu with 5-day waits, 64 ships en route. Saudi Yanbu bypass (4.2M bpd) is the ONLY functioning Gulf export route — Saudi benefits from other Gulf producers being shut out of market. Tadawul +0.2% — muted because security benefit (war ending) vs revenue loss (oil falling) cancel out. CONFIRMED. (Sources: Arab News, WaPo, maritime data, Bloomberg)

Saudi expelled Iranian military staff — persona non grata, citing Article 51 self-defense — effectively reversing the 2023 Beijing Agreement. Jeddah Trilateral Summit (Qatar Emir, MBS, King Abdullah) condemned Iranian attacks on 'vital and civilian facilities.' WaPo: Gulf allies privately pushing Trump to continue — but Bloomberg separately reports 'MBS's Iran War Nightmare: Missiles, Chaos, Threaten Vision 2030.' Saudi position conflicted: suffering from war but seeing 'historic opportunity.' OPEC+ April 5 meeting 2 days away — Saudi +62K bpd planned. (Sources: WaPo, Bloomberg, Gulf News, Al Jazeera)

No new confirmed strikes on Saudi infrastructure this cycle (April 5-6) — positive signal suggesting Saudi may be intercepting more effectively or Iran deprioritizing Saudi targets in favor of Kuwait/UAE. Saudi Tadawul flat at 11,268 (+5.3% monthly) — benefiting from elevated oil prices but capped by physical risk premium. Yanbu bypass still the ONLY functioning Gulf export route but crushed by diverted traffic (~40 VLCCs waiting, 5-day waits). OPEC+ approved 206K bpd increase for May — Saudi leading the symbolic increase but cannot meaningfully deliver while Hormuz closed. OPEC+ warned 'restoring damaged energy assets to full capacity is both costly and takes a long time.' Saudi pre-positioning for post-war pump: once Hormuz reopens, Saudi will flood to recapture market share.

Yanbu congestion worsening: at least 32 supertankers stacked (up from 27). March handled 47 VLCC loadings — 4x pre-war monthly average of 11-12. Port effective throughput ~4M bpd vs pipeline delivery of 7M bpd — port is binding constraint. Saudi informed long-term customers April allocations will be reduced. ENR: 'Hormuz bypass infrastructure was sized for a short disruption. This is not that.' Tadawul/TASI at 11,263 (-0.1%, +7.5% 1M) — Saudi equities benefiting from oil but constrained by bypass bottleneck. OPEC+ confirmed 206K bpd for May — symbolic, most barrels stranded behind Hormuz, cannot be delivered through congested bypass routes. (Sources: House of Saud, Fortune, New Arab, ENR)

SAUDI ARABIA INTERCEPTED 7 BALLISTIC MISSILES OVER EASTERN PROVINCE [CONFIRMED]. Saudi Defense Ministry on-record (April 7) — interception and destruction of seven BMs launched at the Eastern Region (heart of Saudi oil infrastructure). DEBRIS FROM INTERCEPTIONS FELL NEAR ENERGY FACILITIES. The MoD did not specify origin (deliberate ambiguity), but trajectory and Iranian retaliatory pattern make Iran the likely source. Saudi has been notably restrained in war coverage — when they put out an MoD statement naming numbers, the threat is real. Eastern Province is Aramco country. Even successful interceptions create the same 'debris paradox' that shut Borouge down in UAE. The geography of the war is now uniformly hostile to Gulf energy: UAE (Borouge), Bahrain (Bapco), Kuwait (Mina, Shuwaikh, Al Ahmadi), Saudi (Eastern Province) — every Gulf energy producer now under fire or threat.

TASI Saudi Tadawul +7.5% 1M (NOW MOVING) — oil windfall finally pricing. Yanbu bypass monopoly + record SAR oil revenue + Aramco beneficiary chain dominating physical security concerns. The 4-week lag from oil spike to TASI catch-up suggests Saudi institutional investors required confirmation that direct Saudi targeting was off the table. The longer Iran focuses on Kuwait/UAE/Bahrain, the more TASI rallies — but the 7-BM Eastern Province intercept introduces new Saudi-direct risk.

EVENING UPDATE: SABIC Jubail fire — major fire at SABIC plants in Al Jubail Industrial City after Iranian ballistic missile debris fell near facilities. Saudi MoD confirmed 7 ballistic missiles intercepted over Eastern Province. Evacuations ordered, industrial operations suspended to prevent chain reactions. Jubail hosts 150+ plants, 7-12% Saudi GDP, ~50% of FDI in KSA. Most significant Saudi energy infrastructure attack of the war. Rystad Energy: Gulf-wide repair costs minimum $25B. GAP: No confirmed Aramco crude processing damage (petrochemical ≠ crude processing). King Fahd Causeway (Bahrain-Saudi 25km bridge) closed indefinitely due to Iranian attack threats. Saudi overland resupply route to Bahrain cut. OPEC+ 206K bpd for May — Saudi +62K, conditional on Hormuz. (Sources: Arab News, GlobalSecurity/SPA, Insurance Journal, Haaretz, BNN Bloomberg)

IRGC CLAIMS SADARA $20B ARAMCO-DOW JV STRIKE AT JUBAIL — LARGEST SAUDI INDUSTRIAL DAMAGE OF WAR [CONFIRMED]. The IRGC formally claimed responsibility for striking the Jubail industrial hub on April 7 'in response to the enemy's crimes in the aggression against Iran's Asaluyeh petrochemical plants.' Used medium-range missiles + multiple suicide drones. Targets included Sadara Chemical (the $20 billion Saudi Aramco-Dow Chemical JV), additional SABIC plants, and ExxonMobil-linked facilities. Saudi MoD intercepted seven ballistic missiles with debris near energy facilities. Sadara Tadawul disclosure: rated capacity has gone to ZERO overnight — 1.5 Mt/y ethylene, 750 Kt PE, 330 Kt propylene oxide, 400 Kt MDI, all offline with no restart timeline. SABIC Jubail = ~11% of Saudi non-oil GDP and 6-8% of global petrochemical output. Damage is BAKED IN regardless of ceasefire diplomatic outcome.

edges.context: OPEC's de facto leader and only member with significant spare capacity. Only Saudi can meaningfully increase production.
Saudi spare capacity is 3-5M bpd. Yanbu pipeline handling 4.2M bpd. Key source of supply offset.
East-West pipeline to Yanbu bypasses Hormuz for Saudi oil. Critical alternative export route.
Day 40. IRGC claimed Sadara $20B JV strike at Jubail using medium-range missiles + drones. Largest Saudi industrial damage of the war. Damage baked in regardless of ceasefire.

trigger_points.condition: Yanbu pipeline capacity maxed out or sustained attack closes it
trigger_points.mechanism: If both Hormuz AND Yanbu are blocked, Gulf oil has no reliable outlet. Oil could spike to $150+. India would face acute energy crisis.
